title: scirpus pauciflorus var. fernaldii
intro: Scirpus pauciflorus var. fernaldii, also known as Fernald's woolgrass, is a type of grass-like perennial plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. Fernald's woolgrass can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its rhizomes can be dried and used in herbal remedies.
